author: gardener
subject: Daily hygiene pass
summary: Archived 31 historical docs to docs/archive/: layer2 ×7, layer3 ×8, layer4 ×2, layer5-catalog-server, layer5-phase3 ×3, layer5-s2/s3a/s4a/s5a/s6a/s6b ×6, remanence-3c-addendum, spec-v0.1.docx/v0.2/v0.3. Expanded INDEX.md: classified ~25 previously-unlisted docs (current/superseded/historical). No push failures in housekeep.log. Worktree 2a-3-cli-on-new-design at /home/user/remanence-phase2 unchanged.

author: claude
subject: Draft RAO-1: unified Rem Archive Object specification (merges rem-tar-v1 + AOF1 encrypt mode)
summary: Authored docs/rao-v1-specification.md per docs/prompt-merge-rao-spec.md and design-rem-archive-object-format.md. Model: one canonical plaintext object (byte-identical rem-tar-v1, format_id unchanged) with an optional encrypted envelope (magic RAO1, 128-byte plaintext header, AOF1 aead-stream-v1 construction reused verbatim with rao1-* HKDF labels, AEAD chunk = body block so inner BodyLba == AEAD chunk index, footer + zero fill inside stored bytes). plaintext_digest = SHA-256 of canonical bytes (shared across copies; equals plaintext copy's stored_digest); stored_digest external-only. All 12 source-spec conflicts resolved and recorded in Appendix A (chunk size, encryption keyword, digests, metadata layers, framing, labels, naming, raw-v1 dropped, AAD, stored_digest placement). Test vectors: inputs + payload sha256s pinned, layout worked in Appendix C; cryptographic outputs are pinned-at-generation (freeze criterion). Open questions for owner in Appendix D (object_id 64-byte cap, T=C on tape, amber_core parameterization, 3c bootstrap row extension). No crates/ changes.

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 2: drop predecessor compatibility (owner direction) + recommendations on open questions
summary: the owner: prior specs (rem-tar-v1, AOF1) never published or in production; no backwards compat needed; public format is version 1. Revision 2 of docs/rao-v1-specification.md: stream format_id renamed rem-tar-v1 -> rao-v1 (sole wire change; REMTAR1 incorporated by reference AS AMENDED, sec 4.1); AOF1 legacy reader requirement dropped entirely (overrides design doc sec 1.6 - recorded as Appendix A.13); predecessor inputs refused by existing gates; pre-release artifacts to be regenerated; amber retires, only AEAD core absorbed. Appendix D now carries recommendations: dual-implementation pinning of test vectors; keep 64-byte object_id header field; remanence-aead crate with parameterized labels/chunk size, no legacy mode; keep tape-block=chunk_size MUST (one fleet-wide chunk_size); keep caller_object_id confidential; adopt 3c bootstrap row extension; pre-publication editorial merge of the stream spec into one public document. Appendix C arithmetic updated for the shorter format_id record (global pax body 316 -> 312, layout otherwise unchanged).

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 3: apply GPT review findings (keyless geometry, bootstrap leak, layer naming) + design-doc amendments
summary: All 5 GPT findings valid. (1) Envelope geometry IS keyless-derivable (chunk counts spaced C+16, fill absorbs <C, so stored length pins chunk_count uniquely): sec 5.10 now MUSTs positional footer verification via chunk_count=floor((S-144-M)/(C+16)); sec 12.5 states exact size leakage ('encryption hides content, never size'). Formula verified against TV-E1 worked example. (2) Encrypted objects' bootstrap rows MUST NOT carry manifest anchors (manifest size correlates with member count; bootstrap is plaintext on the protected tape) - anchor for decrypted manifests is the authenticated plaintext_digest; recorded as A.14, secs 7.1/8.2/12.5/3.3 updated. (3) design-rem-archive-object-format.md amended in place: dated amendment block at top, sec 1.6 reader-port bullet struck, task B reworded, legacy-read acceptance test dropped. (4) 'Layer 3c parity unchanged' disambiguated in both docs: parity construction/geometry unchanged, bootstrap row schema extended. (5) 'metadata key 3 bytes' -> 'value of metadata map key 3'. Per owner: internal layer numbering removed from the public spec - 'the parity layer' (with one 'Layer 3c in internal documents' parenthetical at first mention and in the REMPARITY reference); recommended names over renumbering to avoid a second drifting scheme.

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 4: second GPT review round (3 findings) + design-doc crypto-detail amendments
summary: (1) sec 6.3 catalog-less encrypted recovery fixed: sequential decrypt to the manifest (final entry) - the 'known inner BodyLba in the bootstrap row' claim contradicted A.14. (2) Keyless error classification downgraded to advisory in sec 5.10: the geometry derivation consumes the input length, so tampered lengths shift chunk_count instead of presenting as surplus; verified numerically (append 306B to TV-E1 -> non-block-multiple with shifted count 6; append one zero block -> self-consistent 6-chunk geometry, InvalidFooter at 24866; forged footer+fill passes structurally and is caught only by stored_digest - now stated under MUST-NOT-claim-authentication). Exact UnexpectedEof/TrailingData classification scoped to keyed readers + catalog-length holders; sec 14.4 negative-vector wording scoped accordingly. (3) Design doc work-order details amended inline: size leakage (exact size derivable), stored_digest external (A.10), keyless inspect surface, empty AAD with structural bindings (A.11); amendment block now states 'where this work order disagrees with the spec, the spec wins' with the divergence list.

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 5: third GPT review round (4 consistency findings)
summary: All small consistency fixes. (1) Encrypted bootstrap row field list unified: exactly the four sec 8.2 envelope fields (representation, key_id, metadata_frame_len, stored block count); object_id removed from the sec 12.5 list - it is recovered from the envelope header in stored block 0. (2) sec 7.1 chain diagram split into off-tape catalog (stored_digest + plaintext_digest per copy) vs on-tape bootstrap row (plaintext: manifest anchors; encrypted: envelope fields only); sec 12.6 anchor wording made ownership-precise (catalog stored_digest, bootstrap manifest_sha256 for plaintext rows). (3) Design doc sec 2.2 plaintext-header list replaced with the spec sec 5.2 field list verbatim (magic RAO1, format_version, suite_id, chunk_size, key_id, hkdf_salt, metadata_frame_len, object_id). (4) Design doc sec 7 grounding: AOF1 spec is 'historical crypto reference only', no legacy reading.

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 6: derived salts replace random salts (salt-robustness hardening, owner-approved)
summary: the owner asked whether 5.4.1's RNG-trusting salt rule was a weakness; analysis: RAO was already softer than the inherited AOF1 wording claimed (object_id in header_hash means cross-object salt reuse was harmless), but the catastrophic window (RNG failure + id reuse) was real. Fix: hkdf_salt is now DERIVED, not random: HKDF(root_key, '', 'rao1-salt-v1' || ctr || object_id_field || plaintext_digest, 16), ctr=0x00 incremented only on the 2^-128 all-zero case. Consequences: identical keys for different plaintexts now require an HMAC-SHA-256 break, not an RNG fault (RNG quality is no longer a confidentiality dependency anywhere); sealing is deterministic (reseal reproduces byte-identical encrypted copy - byte-stable fanout extends to independently sealed copies); the fenced fixed-salt test interface is gone (vectors reproducible by rule); wire format/reader/AEAD untouched. Key-dependent Extract salt justified via TLS 1.3 key-schedule precedent (dual-PRF). Spec changes: 5.4.1 rewritten, 5.4 prop 2 + 5.5.2 + 12.1 narratives corrected (12.1 now states the two structural mechanisms), LABEL_SALT constant, catalog (key_id,salt) uniqueness SHOULD, freeze criterion 11 (determinism + distinctness), TV-E1/TV-D1 salts now derived/pinned-at-generation, B.2 item 9, new A.15 (incl. rejected alternatives: hedged variant, AES-GCM-SIV). Design doc amended: 2.2 construction bullet, 6 nonce-uniqueness checklist line, divergence list.

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 7: crypto review of derived salts (4 findings, 2 high) - metadata bound into salt, v1 drops optional envelope keys, open-time salt verification
summary: GPT round on the Rev 6 salt change, all valid. (1) HIGH: optional envelope metadata (producer/created_unix_seconds/app metadata) broke the zero-nonce argument - reseal with different metadata of same encoded length reproduced the same metadata_key and zero nonce over DIFFERENT plaintext (RFC 8439 violation). Fixed two-layer: v1 defines NO optional keys (metadata = deterministic function of the canonical object; timestamp lives in the canonical global header, app metadata in manifest reserved containers) AND SHA-256(metadata plaintext) is now an input to the salt derivation, so even 1.x extensions or defective sealers cannot reach the reused-nonce state (A.16). (2) HIGH wording: 'requires an HMAC-SHA-256 failure' overclaimed for a 16-byte salt - 12.1/5.4.1/A.15 now state the residual precisely: identifier-reuse bug + 2^-128 truncated-PRF collision, accidental-only (PRF keyed by root_key, ungrindable without it); salt NOT widened (rationale recorded). (3) Keyed open now MUST recompute and verify the salt derivation (new 5.9 step 4, SaltDerivationMismatch error, negative vector, freeze criterion 11 extended) - derivation promoted from writer policy to verified format property. (4) TLS 1.3 wording corrected: not the same construction, rests explicitly on the dual-PRF assumption; RNG claim qualified (sealing consumes no randomness; root-key generation in the registry still needs entropy). Steps renumbered 5.9 (4-8 -> 5-9), cross-refs fixed; A.7 optional-keys note updated; design doc construction bullet amended.

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 8: errata round on Revision 7 (3 findings)
summary: (1) Residual-claim consistency: 5.4 property 2 and the 12.1 intro now carry the same caveat as the corrected residual statement (identifier reuse + 2^-128 truncated-PRF collision; 'closes every operational path... leaving only the quantified residual'). (2) Catalog (key_id, hkdf_salt) uniqueness belt corrected to a consistency check: repeats are legitimate exactly for copies of the same sealed object (same object_id + plaintext_digest, equivalently stored_digest under deterministic sealing) - the previous strict-uniqueness wording would have rejected the byte-stable fanout Revision 6 deliberately enabled. (3) Stale lines fixed: A.15 no longer calls salt provenance 'writer policy, reader untouched' (Revision 7 made it reader-verified); B.2 item 7 no longer says keys 18-20 retained (v1 = required keys 0-3 only).

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 9: residual model gains the SHA-256 collision branch (GPT finding)
summary: Valid finding: the salt derivation sees content only through plaintext_digest (+ v1 metadata, itself a function of size and digest), so two distinct same-size objects with the same object_id and a colliding SHA-256 digest derive identical salts/headers/keys with NO truncated-PRF collision needed - 12.1's 'not a SHA-256 break' was scoped too broadly. 12.1 residual model now has two branches after the mandatory identifier-reuse precondition: (1) SHA-256 digest collision - a primitive break the format already stakes its whole integrity chain on (file_sha256/plaintext_digest/manifest_sha256), so no new assumption; (2) 2^-128 truncated-PRF output collision - truncation bound, accidental-only, reserved for the non-primitive residual as GPT suggested. 5.4 property 2, 5.4.1 property 1, and A.15 carry the same two-branch caveat.

author: claude
subject: RAO spec Revision 10: residual precondition widened to metadata variance; catalog belt requires stored_digest equality
summary: (1) 12.1 residual precondition now reads 'object_id reused across seals that differ in content OR envelope metadata' (v1 metadata cannot differ per object, but the 1.x extension surface can - which is why the model covers it), and branch 1 explicitly includes metadata_hash collisions alongside plaintext_digest; 5.4.1 property 1 parenthetical aligned. (2) Catalog (key_id, hkdf_salt) consistency check now requires agreement on object_id + plaintext_digest + stored_digest - the previous 'equivalently stored_digest' claim fails exactly under the Revision 9 SHA-collision branch; rows agreeing on the first two but differing in stored_digest are now called out as the signature of that residual.

author: claude
subject: RAO: produce clean standalone public spec v1.0 (docs/rao-1.0-specification.md); keep cited working copy
summary: the owner: remove all references to previous work (AOF1, Amber, rem-tar-v1) and present as a fresh v1.0; keep the current cited doc as our reference. Created docs/rao-1.0-specification.md (2564 lines): self-contained, no predecessor citations, no revision history, no resolved-conflicts/changes-from appendices. The plaintext wire format previously incorporated-by-reference to rem-tar-v1-candidate-specification.md is now fully INLINED as normative Section 4 (ustar subset, pax grammar, global header, entries+alignment, manifest+deterministic-CBOR, EOA, writer/reader obligations, standard-tool fallback). format_id rao-v1 throughout. REM-CBOR/RAO-CBOR renamed to neutral 'manifest profile'/'metadata profile' of one deterministic CBOR. Errors merged into one taxonomy (plaintext-stream + envelope). The 'Resolved Conflicts' appendix was rewritten as a clean 'Design Rationale' appendix (B.1-B.13) stating each decision as a 'why' with no merge/conflict framing. Kept: Worked Example (App A), Open Items Before Freeze (App C, trimmed of predecessor/editorial-merge items). REMANENCE.* keyword namespace and uname/gname=remanence retained (implementing project, not previous work). Reference doc rao-v1-specification.md restored from git (Revision 10) and reclassified 'reference' in INDEX; rem-tar-v1 candidate also 'reference'. Verified: zero AOF1/Amber/rem-tar/REMTAR1/revision/conflict/Layer-3c hits in the new doc; cross-refs resolve. Not yet committed.

author: claude
subject: REM-PARITY: standalone public spec v1.0 (docs/rem-parity-1.0-specification.md), payload-agnostic
summary: the owner: official spec for the remanence parity scheme as a standard other archiving tools can implement — a method of writing multiple objects (not necessarily RAOs) to tape; model on rao-1.0. Created docs/rem-parity-1.0-specification.md (1718 lines): self-contained Rem Tape Parity (REM-PARITY) Format v1.0, no predecessor citations (no REM-TAR-1/layer3c/v0.7.2 references; 'epoch' vocabulary throughout). New Section 4 'The Object Contract' makes the format payload-agnostic: objects are opaque block-multiple byte strings, one per tape file; format provides block CRCs + RS parity + addressing + commit + content-blind rediscovery; payload bindings (RAO, plain tar) informative. Wire content carried over from the candidate unchanged, with previously implicit details now pinned normatively from the reference implementation: sidecar index layout algorithm (Section 9.4, greedy no-straddle packing; verified H=3/28648/32767/4121 against the default-geometry test), canonical metadata hash input sequence (domain || header bytes 0x00..0x7F || index entries), parity_map header/footer byte table from parity_map.rs, parity_map sequence semantics (diagnostic, independent counter). All arithmetic vectors (CRC-64/XZ, GF(256)/Cauchy RS, canonical digest) independently re-verified in python before publishing. Appendices: A worked examples (default geometry, sidecar layout, digest byte-walk, minimal tape), B design rationale (11 items incl. frozen bootstrap endianness, interleave, no on-tape commit marker), C open items (image vectors, per-object payload anchor rows coordinated with RAO §8.2/§9 + App C item 4, descriptor-sense classification, filemark-walk scan params, throughput byte-identity). INDEX.md: published spec current, candidate demoted to reference (working copy). rao-1.0 [REMPARITY] reference repointed to the published doc. Candidate's Appendix B implementation-gap backlog deliberately NOT carried into the public doc — it stays in the working copy.

author: claude
subject: REM-PARITY 1.0 spec: external (GPT) review triage — 1 wording fix, 1 new backlog item, 3 already-tracked gaps
summary: GPT review of rem-parity-1.0-specification.md raised 5 findings; all verified against code before acting. (1) parity_map footer magic REM\0PMAPFOOT\x01 vs code reusing the header label: deliberate spec-wins adjudication (candidate App A.4), already backlog item 5, pre-production breaking change — no spec change. (2) footer-inconsistent-with-map primary fallback (spec 13.3 step 2) not implemented (recovery.rs hard-errors): already backlog item 1. (3) directory-assisted tail rescue (spec 13.3 step 3) absent: already backlog item 2. (4) VALID: spec 4.2 said 'every committed object block' has CRC+parity, overstating the pending window W<=o<T — reworded to 'protected' blocks (<W) with explicit pending-epoch sentence referencing 11.2/13.2 and the finish() closure. (5) VALID gap, new: spec 5.3 / candidate 4.3 MUST-reject duplicate-key and non-canonical CBOR is unenforced (ciborium defaults) — added as candidate Appendix B item 11; spec unchanged per specs-are-normative. Net: published spec edit in 4.2 only; candidate backlog +1 item.

author: claude
subject: REM-PARITY 1.0 spec: editorial note now disclaims reference-implementation conformance pre-freeze
summary: Review follow-up: the published doc named Remanence as reference implementation while only Appendix C listed open items, implying the implementation conforms except for App C. Editorial note (remove-at-freeze) now states the reference implementation is the implementation being conformed, with spec-over-implementation gaps in an internal conformance backlog whose closure is Section 18 criterion 1, and clarifies App C holds specification-level items only. Table row kept as 'Reference implementation' to match the rao-1.0 pattern.

author: claude
subject: Tape platform seam: design note (Parts 2-3 deferred); handoff Part 1 CI dependency guard
summary: Brainstorm with owner: can remanence be a format-agnostic tape platform (disk/filesystem analogy), now that REM-PARITY 1.0 is formally a payload-agnostic layout format? Conclusion in docs/tape-platform-seam-design-v0.1.md: the seam already exists (remanence-scsi has zero internal deps; remanence-library depends only on remanence-scsi; catalog coupling to layout+format is correct — fs = object format + catalog, like files and inodes). Parts 2 (drive lease service, device-path grant) and 3 (tape-ownership registry) are sketched but DEFERRED — no current consumer: copy-3 shelf tar is a deliberately independent backend (mtx/mt, separate partition, diversity requirement — must NEVER be migrated onto remanence APIs); dwara2 is partition-isolated. Revival triggers recorded in the note. CODEX TASK (Part 1 only): (a) add a CI check failing if remanence-scsi gains any internal dep or remanence-library gains any internal dep beyond remanence-scsi (assert on parsed Cargo.toml [dependencies]; wire into existing gates); (b) add the platform-contract statement to the workspace README or crates/remanence-library/README.md per the note section 2. No daemon/schema changes. Verify: cargo test + fmt + clippy.

author: codex
subject: Tape platform seam Part 1: dependency guard and README contract
summary: Implemented the approved Part 1 only. Added crates/remanence-library/tests/platform_dependency_guard.rs, an integration test that parses the platform crate Cargo manifests and fails if remanence-scsi depends on any internal crate or remanence-library depends on any internal crate other than remanence-scsi; the test is covered by the existing cargo test CI gate. Added the workspace README platform contract stating that remanence-scsi and remanence-library are format-free standalone platform crates with no REM-PARITY/RAO/catalog/daemon knowledge below the seam. No daemon or schema changes. Verification passed: cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: claude
subject: Amber merge: Amendment 2 (code-merge plan) added to RAO work order; handoff to codex
summary: the owner: merge ~/amber cleanly into remanence so encryption is native; codex implements. Assessment: no new design doc needed — docs/design-rem-archive-object-format.md already IS the work order (tasks B-F); what was missing was the code-level merge map. Added as §10 Amendment 2: (a) normative target = published rao-1.0-specification.md; (b) decision #4 resolved — new crate remanence-aead (no internal deps, independently auditable; amber_cli NOT ported); (c) module-by-module map for amber_core: kdf.rs keep structure + rao1-* labels + NEW salt-derivation fn; seal.rs DROP all RNG machinery incl. fixed_salt_for_test_vectors (spec forbids caller salts, B.11 rejected the test interface) + rewrite to §5.8 workflow; header.rs rewrite to RAO1 128-byte header; metadata.rs to exactly-4-keys §5.5.3; stream.rs parameterize chunk to per-object C, REMOVE short-final-chunk path (full final chunks only); open.rs ADD salt re-derivation check §5.9 step 4 + size/digest step 7 + inner cross-check hooks; inspect/verify to §5.10 keyless geometry; pfr.rs to §6.3/6.4; error.rs to §11.2 names. (d) test-vectors/ + fuzz/ adapt to fixtures/rao/ RAO-TV-P1/E1/D1 + negative suite, pinned-at-generation + independent re-derivation; (e) licensing MIT/Apache->AGPL fine, preserve attribution; (f) sequencing unchanged: ~/amber stays until sutradhara migrates, no AOF1 reader. CODEX TASK: execute work-order tasks B-F per §10. Verify: cargo test/fmt/clippy + §5 acceptance tests + rebuild release binaries before harness runs. Key risk flagged: do not port amber behaviors the spec deliberately changed (random salts, 64KiB fixed chunks, short final chunks).

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: new remanence-aead crate with RAO envelope core
summary: First implementation slice for tasks B-F: added crates/remanence-aead as an isolated crate with no internal Remanence dependencies, and extended the manifest dependency guard so CI fails if that invariant changes. Implemented the RAO 1.0 encrypted-envelope core rather than porting AOF1 public behavior: 128-byte RAO1 header, deterministic rao1-salt-v1 derivation, rao1-object/metadata/payload HKDF split, exact four-key metadata writer with deterministic-CBOR validation, full-chunk-only ChaCha20-Poly1305 STREAM payload, keyed seal/open with salt re-derivation and size/digest verification, footer/fill validation, and keyless inspect geometry. No amber_cli or AOF1 reader was ported. Updated workspace dependencies and README crate list. Remaining work: wire remanence-format plaintext RAO identity and encrypted build/read paths, file-object portability, CLI build/extract/inspect, vectors/fuzz, and acceptance tests. Verification passed: cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO format identity and encrypted object bridge
summary: Second implementation slice for tasks B-F: switched the code-facing plaintext body-format identifier from rem-tar-v1 to rao-v1, wired remanence-format to remanence-aead, and added materializing encrypted RAO write/read helpers. The reader now enforces the global-pax format_id, schema major, encryption=none, and chunk_size contract before payload success, and encrypted read cross-checks the authenticated RAO1 object_id against the inner plaintext stream. Added tests for encrypted round-trip, keyless inspection not exposing inner paths/manifest name, and wrong-key failure before plaintext parsing. API/CLI/state test expectations now use rao-v1. Remaining work: file-object portability, public CLI build/extract/inspect flows, streaming encrypted path, fixtures/vectors/fuzz, and final RAO acceptance coverage. Verification passed: cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: portable RAO file block adapters
summary: Third implementation slice for tasks B-F, focused on Task D portability. Added FileBlockSink and FileBlockSource to remanence-library as fixed-block local object adapters: they expose dense object LBAs, reject partial writes and unaligned source files, provide block_count/len_bytes/cursor helpers, and deliberately reject nonzero filemarks because local RAO object files contain only stored object bytes, not tape filemarks, bootstrap rows, or REM-PARITY sidecars. Exported the adapters, documented the file-object vs tape-object framing in README.md and docs/README.md, and added unit tests for round-trip, block-size enforcement, unaligned input, filemark rejection, locate/space, and tape-like ReadBufferTooSmall behavior. Added remanence-format tests proving plaintext rao-v1 and encrypted RAO1 objects round-trip through the file adapters. Also cleaned remaining code-facing rem-tar terminology in comments/error messages. Remaining work: public CLI build/extract/inspect flows, streaming encrypted path, fixtures/vectors/fuzz, range/PFR acceptance coverage, and bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object envelope rows. Verification passed: cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: claude
subject: RAO: decision to add symlinks + empty dirs via native ustar typeflags (pre-freeze); design note written, spec/impl deferred
summary: Brainstorm with owner: media/photo workflow has empty dirs, symlinks (resolving + dangling), and Mac Finder color tags. Verified factually (web): Mac color labels ARE xattrs (com.apple.metadata:_kMDItemUserTags binary plist label+color 0-7; legacy color in com.apple.FinderInfo); off Mac-native filesystems they externalize as ._ AppleDouble sidecar 'hidden files' (NOT .DS_Store, which is folder view state). DECISION: encode symlinks + empty dirs natively in RAO 1.0 using standard ustar typeflags 2 (symlink) and 5 (directory), expanding the current regular-files-only scope BEFORE freeze (post-freeze it would be a v2 change per spec §10; pre-freeze it's free since no RAO reader is deployed). Chose native typeflags over manifest-annotation because stock tar then restores links/dirs faithfully (the longevity net). Wrote docs/rao-nonregular-entries-design-v0.1.md as the driving record: wire changes (typeflags, linkname/pax linkpath, dir path convention), manifest additions (entry_type + link_target, absent-by-default so regular-file objects stay byte-identical / plaintext_digest unchanged), a recommended alignment refinement (exempt zero-payload entries from chunk-alignment to avoid burning ~256KiB padding per tiny entry), symlink restore traversal-security expansion (§12.10), encryption unaffected, and a per-section spec-edit checklist. Sub-decisions left for spec-edit time: dir trailing-slash spelling, file_sha256 for non-regular (lean: absent), empty-only vs all dirs (lean: empty-only), entry_type encoding. Dangling symlinks need no special case (store the target string). Hardlinks explicitly out of scope. STILL OPEN (separate, next): Mac xattr capture policy (metadata_preservation_data container vs treat ._ files as payload vs wrap-then-store). DEFERRED: spec edit + impl are later work; coordinate impl with the in-flight amber merge (Amendment 2) since both touch remanence-format writer/reader. No codex handoff yet.

author: codex
subject: RAO: native symlink and empty-directory entries plus local archive build
summary: Implemented the latest approved RAO nonregular-entry decision and continued the Amber merge public build slice. remanence-format now models regular files, symlinks, and directories; emits ustar typeflags 0/2/5; supports pax linkpath; omits file_sha256 for nonregular entries; records manifest entry_type/link_target only when needed; exempts zero-payload entries from chunk-alignment padding; and reads/writes symlinks, dangling symlinks, and empty directories. docs/rao-1.0-specification.md now reflects the expanded pre-freeze scope, path rules, manifest fields, reader dispatch, standard tar extraction behavior, and restore safety expectations. remanence-cli now exposes rem archive build as a discovery-free local RAO object builder for plaintext or encrypted objects, with deterministic optional test knobs, JSON reports, streaming regular-file readers, empty-directory inclusion, and no symlink following. Remaining Amber merge work: extract/inspect CLI surfaces, fixtures/vectors/fuzz, range/PFR acceptance coverage, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, and full restore policy for symlink materialization. Verification passed: cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: local RAO inspect/extract and nonregular restore
summary: Continued the Amber merge public CLI slice. remanence-cli now exposes discovery-free rem archive inspect and rem archive extract for portable RAO object files: plaintext inspect/extract through FileBlockSource, encrypted keyless inspect, encrypted keyed inspect with inner plaintext details, and encrypted extract after authenticated open and inner object_id cross-check. remanence-stream restore_object_to_directory now handles the full pre-freeze RAO entry set by creating empty directories and symlinks without following symlink targets, while preserving existing regular-file hash verification and traversal guards. Added parser, help, plaintext, encrypted, and symlink/empty-directory restore coverage. Encrypted extract is still materialized rather than streaming; remaining Amber merge work includes fixtures/vectors/fuzz, PFR/range acceptance, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, and live harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: gemini
subject: CI fix: stable Rust migration and 1.85 MSRV update
summary: Investigated and fixed CI failures caused by modern dependency updates (clap 4.6, time 0.3.47) requiring Cargo's 'edition2024' feature, which is only available in Rust 1.85+. Updated .github/workflows/ci.yml to use the 'stable' Rust toolchain instead of pinned 1.80.0, and increased the workspace 'rust-version' to 1.85 in Cargo.toml. Verified that the current dependency set in Cargo.lock now compiles and passes all tests on stable. Also confirmed and committed pending orchestration changes in remanence-stream for symlink and directory restore, completing the 'Amber merge' slice implementation. Verification passed: cargo test --workspace; cargo clippy --workspace; cargo fmt --check.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: encrypted RAO file-range PFR primitive
summary: Implemented the next Amber merge PFR slice. remanence-aead now exposes keyed encrypted-range openers that authenticate the metadata frame, map canonical plaintext offsets or inner BodyLba-relative ranges to Section 6 ciphertext ranges, decrypt only the fetched AEAD chunks with finality computed from authenticated plaintext_size, and return a RangeOpenReport with stored-range evidence. remanence-format now exposes read_encrypted_rao_file_range_to_vec, validating catalog/manifest file rows before delegating to the envelope layer. Tests cover chunk-boundary trimming, BodyLba mapping, zero-length ranges, successful PFR despite damage in an unrequested encrypted chunk, and fail-closed behavior when a requested chunk tag is damaged. Also applied mechanical stable-toolchain clippy fixes in remanence-chaos/remanence-parity. Remaining Amber merge work includes Section 13 fixtures/vectors and independent re-derivation, fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, and live harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: initial RAO Section 13 vector manifests
summary: Implemented the first Section 13 fixture/vector slice from the remaining Amber merge backlog. Added fixtures/rao manifests for RAO-TV-P1, RAO-TV-E1, and RAO-TV-D1 with reference-implementation pinned-at-generation values: P1 layout, file payload hashes, manifest CBOR/hash, stored digest, first block digest; E1 deterministic salt/header/header-hash/keys/metadata frame/payload-frame hash/stored digest and the required plaintext_digest == P1 stored_digest equality; D1 default-chunk plaintext and encrypted geometry/pinned values. Added remanence-format's rao_vectors integration test, which rebuilds the vectors from the normative inputs and compares the manifests. Independent re-derivation remains pending before freeze, as recorded in the manifests; remaining Amber merge work includes the negative vector suite, fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, and live harness coverage. Verification passed: jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: initial RAO negative vector manifests
summary: Implemented the first executable Section 13.5 negative-vector slice. Added fixtures/rao negative manifests for plaintext writer-side cases (duplicate path/file_id, manifest file_id collision, reserved/control/noncanonical paths, malformed mtime, streamed wrong hash/size, invalid chunk size, symlink/directory zero-payload contract faults) and encrypted envelope header/framing/crypto-tamper/writer cases. Added rao_negative_vectors integration tests that execute every manifest case and assert the normative Section 11 error name. Fixed two writer-side conformance gaps surfaced by the suite: payload file_id now must be distinct from the generated manifest file_id, and mtime now validates the POSIX pax decimal-seconds shape. The negative manifests are explicitly marked partial; remaining negative work includes byte-level plaintext reader vectors, manifest-profile malformed-CBOR vectors, encrypted metadata defective-sealer vectors including SaltDerivationMismatch and PlaintextDigestMismatch constructions, plus fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, and live harness coverage. Verification passed: jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO defective-sealer negative vectors
summary: Extended the executable RAO Section 13.5 encrypted-envelope negative suite with the two previously named defective-sealer binding cases. fixtures/rao/negative-envelope.json now includes an authenticated, salt-consistent object whose metadata deliberately misstates plaintext_digest (expected PlaintextDigestMismatch) and an otherwise self-consistent object sealed under a non-derived header salt (expected SaltDerivationMismatch). The rao_negative_vectors test gained a small defective_envelope builder using public RAO header/KDF/STREAM primitives so these are byte-level authenticated negative cases rather than header-tamper shortcuts. The negative manifests remain partial; remaining work includes byte-level plaintext reader vectors, manifest-profile malformed-CBOR vectors, other encrypted envelope negative families such as wrong-final-flag and metadata-profile repertoire cases, fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, live harness coverage, and independent re-derivation of positive vectors. Verification passed: jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO envelope metadata negative vectors
summary: Extended the executable RAO Section 13.5 encrypted-envelope negative suite from 30 to 41 cases. Added the wrong-final-flag STREAM case plus authenticated metadata-profile cases for float, tag, indefinite-length item, duplicate key, non-shortest integer, missing plaintext_size, metadata_version 2, plaintext_size zero, plaintext_size not multiple of chunk_size, and overflow-implying plaintext_size. The rao_negative_vectors helper now builds encrypted envelopes from arbitrary metadata plaintext while keeping header parsing and metadata AEAD authentication successful, so the vectors assert the intended metadata error names. Fixed the reference implementation conformance gap for overflow-implying plaintext_size by making RaoMetadata::validate reject payload-frame arithmetic overflow as InvalidMetadataField before open can enter impossible payload loops. The negative manifests remain partial; remaining work includes byte-level plaintext reader vectors, manifest-profile malformed-CBOR vectors, inner cross-check defective-sealer vectors, fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, live harness coverage, and independent re-derivation of positive vectors. Verification passed: jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-aead metadata_rejects_bad_plaintext_size; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO manifest-profile negative vectors
summary: Implemented the next Section 13.5 manifest-profile slice from the remaining Amber merge backlog. Added remanence-format manifest validation over the original CBOR bytes before schema interpretation: manifest entry bytes are checked against the pax REMANENCE.file_sha256 anchor, deterministic encoding rejects non-shortest integers/lengths, non-text/duplicate/unsorted map keys, indefinite-length items, tags, floats, unsupported simple values, trailing bytes, invalid UTF-8, and excessive nesting, and schema checks cover the required top-level fields plus per-entry required fields and file_sha256 width. Materializing and streaming RAO readers now validate the manifest before returning success, with a reader regression for manifest anchor mismatch. Added fixtures/rao/negative-manifest.json with 12 executable cases including the accepted unknown-key extension case, and updated the fixture README remaining-work list. The negative manifests remain partial; remaining work includes byte-level plaintext reader vectors, inner cross-check defective-sealer vectors, fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, live harness coverage, and independent re-derivation of positive vectors. Verification passed: jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-format manifest_negative_vectors_match_manifest_errors -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-format reader_rejects_manifest_digest_mismatch -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO plaintext reader negative vectors
summary: Implemented the Section 13.5 byte-level plaintext reader negative-vector slice. Added fixtures/rao/negative-plaintext-reader.json with 20 executable cases: wrong format_id, schema major 2, missing/unsupported compression, non-none inner encryption, chunk_size mismatch, bad ustar checksum, single-zero EOF, unknown typeflag, misaligned payload, traversal-shaped path, entry after manifest, restore digest mismatch, truncated payload, truncated pax body, and pax grammar/UTF-8 faults. Extended rao_negative_vectors with byte-level tar/pax mutation helpers, checksum recalculation, pax padding rebalancing, manifest-after-entry insertion, and a restore-mode digest-checking sink. Fixed conformance gaps surfaced by the vectors: readers now reject entries after the manifest, chunk-size disagreement returns ChunkSizeMismatch, reader-side canonical path failures return InvalidPath, and restore hash failures return FileDigestMismatch instead of generic Parse. The negative manifests remain partial; remaining work includes inner cross-check defective-sealer vectors, additional encrypted envelope families, fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, live harness coverage, and independent re-derivation of positive vectors. Verification passed: jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors plaintext_reader_negative_vectors_match_manifest_errors -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO encrypted inner cross-check vectors
summary: Implemented the next Section 13.5 negative-vector slice called out by the latest journal entry. Added fixtures/rao/negative-inner.json with three authenticated defective-sealer cases: envelope header object_id differs from inner REMANENCE.object_id, inner REMANENCE.chunk_size differs from the encrypted header chunk_size, and inner REMANENCE.encryption is non-none. read_encrypted_rao_object now maps these decrypted-inner object_id/chunk_size/format-gate failures to the spec-named InnerObjectMismatch error. The rao_negative_vectors test seals each modified plaintext with a matching digest and coherent AEAD metadata, so the envelope authenticates before the inner cross-check fails. The negative manifests remain partial; remaining work includes additional encrypted envelope families, fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, live harness coverage, and independent re-derivation of positive vectors. Verification passed: jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors encrypted_inner_negative_vectors_match_manifest_errors -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO envelope metadata-schema vectors
summary: Extended the RAO Section 13.5 encrypted-envelope negative suite with authenticated metadata-schema and metadata-profile edge cases: non-map top-level metadata, non-unsigned top-level key, forbidden undefined simple value, trailing bytes, missing required keys 0/2/3, wrong types for metadata_version/plaintext_size/plaintext_digest_alg/plaintext_digest, non-sha256 digest algorithm, and a 31-byte plaintext_digest. Fixed the reference implementation taxonomy so non-unsigned top-level metadata keys return InvalidCborEncoding per Section 5.5.3, while defined-key wrong values still return InvalidMetadataField. Added a positive decoder regression proving unknown unsigned metadata extension keys with profile-valid values are accepted and ignored. Remaining Amber merge work includes fuzz targets, bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, live harness coverage, and independent re-derivation of positive vectors. Verification passed: jq empty journal/2026-06-11.json fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo test -p remanence-aead metadata_ -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors envelope_negative_vectors_match_manifest_errors -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-format --test rao_negative_vectors -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO fuzz target harness
summary: Implemented the first RAO Section 14.8 fuzzing slice by adding an in-repo cargo-fuzz package with targets for the encrypted envelope header parser, encrypted metadata CBOR decoder, manifest CBOR profile decoder, plaintext tar/pax record loop, and whole-object open/verify classification path. Added a fuzzing feature to remanence-format that exposes only the manifest deterministic-CBOR profile validator for the fuzz harness; production manifest validation remains unchanged. The fuzz targets cap input sizes and ignore expected parse errors so crashes, panics, hangs, and allocation issues remain findings. This satisfies the missing target scaffolding but not the freeze requirement to run coverage-guided fuzzing to a corpus plateau. Remaining Amber merge work includes bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance, live harness coverage, independent re-derivation of positive vectors, and actual fuzz campaign/plateau evidence. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fuzz list; cargo +nightly fuzz check; cargo test -p remanence-format --features fuzzing; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo clippy -p remanence-format --features fuzzing -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO parity-over-ciphertext recovery acceptance
summary: Implemented the RAO Section 14.6 parity-over-ciphertext acceptance slice. Extended the Layer 3b/3c integration test fixture with physical-LBA erasure injection over preserved tape filemark layout, then added an encrypted RAO object written through ParitySink. The test corrupts one protected encrypted stored block, proves a clean-read tampered object fails AEAD authentication before repair, proves ObjectParitySource can recover the ciphertext block without any RAO key via recover_block_at, and proves keyed read_encrypted_rao_object succeeds when the same damaged physical block is reported as unreadable and reconstructed by REM-PARITY. Remaining Amber merge work includes bootstrap/catalog encrypted-object rows, live harness coverage, independent re-derivation of positive vectors, and actual fuzz campaign/plateau evidence. Verification passed: cargo test -p remanence-format --test layer3c_integration -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO encrypted copy catalog rows
summary: Implemented the catalog half of the RAO Section 8.2 encrypted-object row requirement. Bumped the Layer 4 SQLite schema to 7 and added object_copies columns for representation, key_id, and metadata_frame_len, with plaintext defaults for migrated rows and validation that encrypted rows carry a nonzero 16-byte key_id plus positive metadata_frame_len while plaintext rows carry no encrypted envelope fields. Extended NativeObjectCopyRecord and NativeObjectCopyProjectionInput, re-exported representation constants, and updated Layer 5 plaintext pool-write projections/tests accordingly. The live combined object+bundle projection now treats bundle-derived tape-file rows as locator-only updates so they preserve already-projected encrypted envelope fields instead of clobbering them back to plaintext defaults. Added a focused state test proving an encrypted RAO copy row survives the live projection ordering and round-trips through SQLite. The bootstrap CBOR per-object row schema remains open because REM-PARITY reserves that extension surface to be specified jointly with RAO; this pass does not invent an incompatible bootstrap wire layout. Remaining Amber merge work includes bootstrap per-object row schema/plumbing, live harness coverage, independent re-derivation of positive vectors, and actual fuzz campaign/plateau evidence. Verification passed: cargo test -p remanence-state encrypted_native_object_copy_projection_persists_envelope_fields -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-state -- --nocapture; cargo test; cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO bootstrap object rows
summary: Implemented the bootstrap half of the RAO Section 8.2 object-row requirement. REM-PARITY bootstrap schema minor is now 2 and key 30 carries strictly ordered per-object rows: plaintext rows carry manifest_first_chunk_lba, manifest_size_bytes, manifest_chunk_count, and manifest_sha256; encrypted rows carry key_id and metadata_frame_len only. ParitySink exposes record_bootstrap_object_row, includes rows in checkpoint/final bootstrap payloads, and preserves them in object journal bundles; the streaming plaintext path records manifest anchors, and the encrypted Layer 3b/3c acceptance test now records and checks the encrypted envelope row. Journal replay projects encrypted rows into Layer 4 object_copies representation/key_id/metadata_frame_len while preserving old locator-only bundle behavior when no row is present. Updated rao-1.0 and rem-parity-1.0 to specify the row schema and removed the resolved bootstrap-extension open item. Remaining Amber merge work includes live harness coverage, independent re-derivation of positive vectors, and actual fuzz campaign/plateau evidence. Verification passed: cargo test -p remanence-parity bootstrap -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-parity file_journal_round_trips_committed_bundle -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-stream writes_catalog_projection_and_restores_to_filesystem -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-state journal_bootstrap_object_row_projects_encrypted_copy_fields -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-format --test layer3c_integration encrypted_rao_ciphertext_recovers_through_parity_before_keyed_open -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: independent RAO positive vector re-derivation
summary: Implemented the independent positive-vector re-derivation slice from the round 18 Amber backlog. Added tools/verify_rao_vectors_independent.py, a Python verifier that avoids the Remanence Rust crates and rebuilds the RAO-TV-P1 and RAO-TV-D1 plaintext tar/PAX streams, deterministic manifest CBOR, and RAO-TV-E1/D1 encrypted envelopes using only Python code plus cryptography's ChaCha20-Poly1305. The verifier checks pinned payload hashes, layout offsets, manifest bytes/hash, encrypted header fields, HKDF salt and derived keys, metadata frame, payload-frame digest, stored digest, and plaintext_digest equality. Marked RAO-TV-P1, RAO-TV-E1, and RAO-TV-D1 independent_rederivation as verified and documented the verifier dependency in fixtures/rao/README.md. Remaining Amber merge work includes live harness coverage and actual fuzz campaign/plateau evidence. Verification passed: python3 -B tools/verify_rao_vectors_independent.py; jq empty fixtures/rao/*.json; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: dictionary-backed RAO fuzz campaign runner
summary: Implemented the next fuzzing infrastructure slice for RAO Section 14 criterion 8. Added checked-in libFuzzer dictionaries for all five RAO fuzz targets (envelope header, envelope metadata CBOR, manifest CBOR, plaintext tar/PAX loop, and whole-object open/verify) plus tools/run_rao_fuzz_campaign.sh, a sequential campaign runner that passes each dictionary explicitly, runs cargo +nightly fuzz check, executes each target for a configurable max_total_time, and then replays the saved corpus with -runs=0 for compact final coverage/corpus stats. A dictionary-backed campaign made real progress and found no crashes or artifacts, but it also found new coverage, so criterion 8 is intentionally NOT closed yet: final replay snapshots after this pass were header cov 221/ft 242, metadata cov 170/ft 530, manifest cov 132/ft 545, tar loop cov 193/ft 298, and whole-object cov 626/ft 723. Remaining Amber merge work includes actual fuzz campaign/plateau evidence using the enriched corpora and live harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fuzz check; per-target dictionary replay with -runs=0; FUZZ_VERBOSITY=0 tools/run_rao_fuzz_campaign.sh 1; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO fuzz campaign continuation
summary: Continued the RAO Section 14 criterion 8 coverage-guided fuzz campaign with the dictionary-backed runner. No target crashed, hung, produced an artifact, or showed unbounded allocation behavior. Header completed a 180-second pass with 92,342,771 executions, new_units_added 0, replay cov 221/ft 242. Metadata completed 16,036,767 executions, replay cov 170/ft 530. Manifest completed 22,824,292 executions, replay cov 132/ft 545. The tar/PAX loop completed 21,239,172 executions, new_units_added 0, replay cov 193/ft 298. Whole-object open/verify remained the limiting target: focused runs of 14,088,944, 12,427,834, 19,769,179, and 18,209,026 executions advanced replay coverage from cov 626/ft 723 to cov 1105/ft 1470, with the final pass still adding one feature. Added the newly rediscovered near-magic and numeric tokens to rao_whole_object_open_verify.dict to speed the next plateau attempt. Criterion 8 therefore remains open, deliberately: the enriched whole-object corpus is still moving. Remaining Amber merge work includes actual fuzz plateau evidence and live harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -runs=0 -print_final_stats=1 -dict=...; jq empty journal/2026-06-11.json; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: CLI encrypted RAO range extraction
summary: Implemented the CLI slice for the RAO PFR acceptance path. rem archive extract now accepts catalog/build-report-backed range arguments (--path, --first-chunk-lba, --file-size-bytes, --range start:length) in addition to the existing full-object restore path. Plaintext range extraction validates and slices the parsed member; encrypted range extraction uses remanence-format's RAO PFR bridge and remanence-aead's chunk range opener, so it authenticates and decrypts only the encrypted chunks covering the requested member range. The new CLI report includes mode=range, member path, file size, requested range, bytes written, key id, and authenticated/stored chunk-range metadata for harness assertions. Added a regression that builds an encrypted RAO, corrupts an unrequested encrypted chunk so full keyed open fails, then proves rem archive extract --range still restores the requested bytes. This advances RAO Section 14 criterion 5 and the design-doc byte-range acceptance test; remaining Amber merge work still includes actual fuzz plateau evidence and live harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo test -p remanence-format encrypted_file_range_ -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-cli rem_archive_extract_parses_local_file_range_command -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-cli archive_extract_encrypted_range_uses_covering_chunks_only -- --nocapture; cargo test -p remanence-cli -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO whole-object fuzz corpus expansion
summary: Ran another focused RAO Section 14 criterion 8 attempt against rao_whole_object_open_verify after the prior dictionary enrichment. Baseline replay was cov 1105/ft 1470 with Dictionary: 20 entries and no artifacts. The 300-second run completed 17,524,234 executions with no crash, hang, artifact, or unbounded allocation signal, peak RSS 110 MiB, but it added 92 new corpus units and advanced the target to cov 1130/ft 1531, including new function coverage. Therefore criterion 8 remains deliberately open: the whole-object corpus is still moving. Added the run's recommended whole-object dictionary tokens (u64 2421, little-endian u32 13, repeated 0x0d, RAOR, and *AO1). Replay with the enriched 25-entry dictionary succeeded at cov 1130/ft 1531 and no artifacts. Remaining Amber merge work still includes actual fuzz plateau evidence and live harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -runs=0 -print_final_stats=1 -dict=... before and after the run; cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -max_total_time=300 -print_final_stats=1 -verbosity=1 -dict=...; cargo +nightly fuzz check.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO whole-object fuzz corpus still moving
summary: Continued the RAO Section 14 criterion 8 attempt against rao_whole_object_open_verify using the enriched whole-object dictionary. Baseline replay with Dictionary: 25 entries was cov 1130/ft 1531 with no artifacts. A 600-second focused run completed 48,021,262 executions with no crash, hang, artifact, or unbounded allocation signal, peak RSS 163 MiB, but it added new corpus units and advanced the target to cov 1130/ft 1534. Therefore criterion 8 remains deliberately open: the whole-object corpus is still moving. Added the run's recommended dictionary operands (RAOk, u64 8, u64 355, u32 49, u64 1183, little-endian u32 10, and u64 -71) so the next attempt starts from the newly discovered comparisons. Replay with the enriched 32-entry dictionary succeeded at cov 1130/ft 1534 and no artifacts. The system harness also now has pushed local RAO portable-object coverage in system commit 005a437 (scenario-rao: plaintext/encrypted RAO at 4 KiB and 256 KiB chunk sizes, standard tar fallback, keyed/keyless checks, encrypted range extraction), but live QuadStor/MSL3040 coverage remains open. Remaining Amber merge work still includes actual fuzz plateau evidence and live tape harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -runs=0 -print_final_stats=1 -dict=... before and after the run; cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -max_total_time=600 -print_final_stats=1 -verbosity=1 -dict=...; cargo +nightly fuzz check; jq empty journal/2026-06-11.json; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO whole-object fuzz plateau attempt still moves
summary: Continued the RAO Section 14 criterion 8 plateau attempt against rao_whole_object_open_verify using the 32-entry whole-object dictionary. Baseline replay was cov 1130/ft 1534 with 270 corpus files and no artifacts. A 900-second focused run completed 47,611,314 executions with no crash, hang, artifact, or unbounded allocation signal, peak RSS 121 MiB, but it added 5 new corpus units and advanced the target to cov 1133/ft 1537. Therefore criterion 8 remains deliberately open: the whole-object corpus is still moving. Added the run's recommended comparison operands (little-endian u64 31 and u64 1104) so the next attempt starts from the newly discovered comparisons. Replay with the enriched 34-entry dictionary and corpus succeeded at cov 1133/ft 1537 and no artifacts. Remaining Amber merge work still includes actual fuzz plateau evidence and live QuadStor/MSL3040 tape harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -runs=0 -print_final_stats=1 -dict=... before and after the run; cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -max_total_time=900 -print_final_stats=1 -verbosity=1 -dict=...; jq empty journal/2026-06-11.json; cargo +nightly fuzz check; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO whole-object fuzz coverage plateau evidence
summary: Continued the RAO Section 14 criterion 8 plateau attempt against rao_whole_object_open_verify using the 34-entry whole-object dictionary. Baseline replay was cov 1133/ft 1537 with 275 corpus files and no artifacts. A 1200-second focused run completed 86,666,509 executions with no crash, hang, artifact, or unbounded allocation signal, peak RSS 133 MiB, and did not advance coverage or feature count: final cov/ft remained 1133/1537. LibFuzzer still performed corpus reduction work (stat::new_units_added 6) and recommended two comparison operands, so this is strong whole-object coverage-plateau evidence but not recorded as final Section 14 criterion 8 closure. Added the recommended operands (u64 601 and a zero-prefix plus RAO1/header-length probe) so the next pass starts from those comparisons. Replay with the enriched 36-entry dictionary succeeded at cov 1133/ft 1537 and no artifacts. Remaining Amber merge work still includes final all-target fuzz plateau consolidation and live QuadStor/MSL3040 tape harness coverage. Verification passed: cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -runs=0 -print_final_stats=1 -dict=... before and after the run; cargo +nightly fuzz run rao_whole_object_open_verify -- -max_total_time=1200 -print_final_stats=1 -verbosity=1 -dict=...; jq empty journal/2026-06-11.json; cargo +nightly fuzz check; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO all-target fuzz plateau consolidation
summary: Closed the local RAO Section 14 criterion 8 fuzz-evidence gap with an all-target consolidation pass over the current corpora and checked-in dictionaries. The standard 300-second-per-target campaign produced no crashes, hangs, artifacts, or unbounded allocation signals. Header completed 153,205,441 executions at cov 221/ft 242 with new_units_added 0 and replay cov 221/ft 242; envelope metadata CBOR completed 24,827,675 executions at cov 170/ft 530 with reduction-only corpus churn and replay cov 170/ft 530; manifest CBOR completed 38,045,451 executions at cov 132/ft 546 with reduction-only churn and subsequent replay cov 132/ft 546; plaintext tar loop completed 35,178,717 executions at cov 193/ft 298 with new_units_added 0 and replay cov 193/ft 298; whole-object open/verify completed 22,116,965 executions at cov 1133/ft 1537 with reduction-only churn and replay cov 1133/ft 1537. To separate real feature discovery from corpus minimization, reran all five targets for 300 seconds each with -reduce_inputs=0: header 163,829,511 executions, metadata 25,690,028, manifest 38,650,602, tar loop 36,547,052, and whole-object 23,485,861; every target finished with stat::new_units_added 0 and replayed at the same cov/ft values (221/242, 170/530, 132/546, 193/298, 1133/1537). No dictionary edits were made from this pass because the no-reduction confirmation produced no new comparison recommendations. Remaining Amber merge work is now live QuadStor/MSL3040 tape harness coverage for both plaintext and encrypted RAO representations at two chunk sizes. Verification passed: FUZZ_VERBOSITY=1 tools/run_rao_fuzz_campaign.sh 300; per-target cargo +nightly fuzz run ... -max_total_time=300 -reduce_inputs=0 -print_final_stats=1 -dict=... plus replay; find fuzz/artifacts -maxdepth 2 -type f produced no output; jq empty journal/2026-06-11.json; cargo +nightly fuzz check; cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release.

author: codex
subject: Amber merge: RAO live QuadStor tape harness coverage
summary: Implemented the live QuadStor RAO tape harness slice for the remaining Amber merge work. Remanence now supports pool-targeted encrypted RAO tape writes with RAO1 sealing, encrypted catalog/bootstrap envelope fields, keyed encrypted tape reads/verifies, and exact stored-footprint capacity guarding before tape I/O. The system harness now has RAO-LIVE, covering plaintext and encrypted RAO tape round trips at 4 KiB and 256 KiB block sizes on dedicated RMR QuadStor media, plus repeatable VTL reset/recycle scripts for those cartridges. The final live run report was reports/2026-06-11-235648-rao-live-quadstor-tape-rao-acceptance.json with RAOL.3 and RAOL.4 both ok. This closes the QuadStor half of the live tape coverage gap; physical MSL3040 coverage remains separate because no physical MSL3040 target was visible in this environment. Verification passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo test; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo build --release; bringup/vtl-rao-cartridges.sh; bringup/rem-init.sh; bringup/recycle-scenario-rao-live-tapes.sh; make scenario-rao-live; uv run pytest -q; git diff --check.
